title: SRS Airbag System Structure - Overview
section: Driver assistance SRS airbags seat belts

blocks.text: All models are equipped with a driver's side airbag, passenger side airbag, and seatbelt pretensioner. In addition, side airbags and side curtain airbags are equipped depending on the model.

In the event of a frontal collision, in addition to the impact-absorbing structure of the vehicle body, it is equipped with a function to mitigate the impact to the head and chest of the front seat occupant in combination with a body restraint system using a seat belt pretensioner. In addition, the driver's seat airbag uses a continuous volume change type that both improves occupant protection performance and reduces the impact force of the airbag.

The seat belt pretensioner further enhances the effectiveness of the seat belt by momentarily retracting the seat belt in the event of a collision to firmly secure the occupant in the seat. In the event of a low-speed collision, the seat belt pretensioner is activated independently because the seat belt alone is sufficient to secure occupant restraint. In the event of a low-to-medium-speed or high-speed collision, the SRS airbags and seat belt pretensioners are activated to ensure even greater occupant restraint and protection.

3. In the collision determination circuit, an activation signal is output when the output of the front impact sensor and the positive impact G sensor exceeds a set threshold value. If the output of the safing drive circuit in the SRS unit is above the set threshold, current flows to the ignition circuit and the SRS airbags and seat belt pretensioners are activated (deployed).
It is activated at speeds of approximately 20-30 km/h or more in the event of a head-on collision with a fixed wall of extremely thick concrete.

items.text: The system is activated when two vehicles collide head-on at approximately 20-30 km/h or more.

In the event of a head-on collision with a stationary vehicle, the system is activated at approximately twice the speed (approximately 40-60 km/h or more). However, the operating conditions will vary depending on the vehicle and conditions (angle, etc.) of the collision.

blocks.text: The SRS airbags are triggered when the vehicle is subjected to a strong impact from a direction within 30° to the left or right in front of the vehicle while the ignition is in the ON mode. Therefore, in the case shown in the figure, even if the SRS airbag deploys, it will not be effective in protecting occupants, so it will not be activated. However, the SRS airbags may be triggered depending on the collision situation.

Since the SRS airbags are triggered depending on the magnitude of the impact suffered by the occupant, the size of the damage to the vehicle does not necessarily coincide with the activation of the SRS airbags.
Side airbag systems and side curtain airbag systems

In the event of a side impact collision, in addition to the impact-absorbing structure of the vehicle body, the side airbags, side curtain airbags, and seatbelt pretensioners on the side of the collision are activated to mitigate the impact to the occupants' head and chest. Side impact sensors located on the left and right sides of the vehicle body allow the airbags to deploy in response to various types of side collisions. The side airbags are of the variable volume type, and the structure of the airbag pressure chamber and the deployment process have been improved to enable safe deployment regardless of the occupant's posture. This eliminates the need for OPDS* related parts.

3. An activation signal is sent from the collision detection circuit to the ignition circuit to activate (deploy) the side airbags, side curtain airbags, and seat belt pretensioners. In the event of a strong impact from the front, the side curtain airbags may be activated (deployed) if the side judgment in the SRS unit exceeds a set threshold value.

If the vehicle is subjected to a strong side impact and the lateral G-sensor detects an impact greater than a set value, the side airbag and side curtain airbag on the side of the impacted vehicle are activated. However, the side curtain airbags may be triggered together with the front airbags to protect the occupants' heads in the event of a frontal collision with a certain amount of lateral force.

Side airbags and side curtain airbags are designed to be activated when the vehicle is subjected to a side impact greater than a set value. Even if the vehicle is subjected to a side impact, they may not be activated if the impact is mitigated by the vehicle's shock absorbing structure or if the vehicle is side impacted by a structure other than the passenger compartment. Although it may be activated in the event of a sideslip collision or when the vehicle overturns, it does not provide the original occupant protection effect.
